title: PM Lane 163 - Local Field Prep Output Subgrouping
objective: Reduce Project Miner Temp Power output-action scan burden by grouping the existing 19 Field Prep Outputs into Field Prep Basics, Admission Drafts, and Pilot Launch Outputs without changing button labels, handlers, filenames, payloads, storage, read seams, or write boundaries.
changes: Grouped the existing Field Prep Outputs rail into Field Prep Basics, Admission Drafts, and Pilot Launch Outputs.
Preserved all 19 existing field-prep output buttons, labels, disabled states, handlers, filenames, and payloads.

Extended the focused PM import-intake smoke to prove the subgroup headings, subgroup button counts, exact subgroup button labels, unchanged total field-prep button count, disclosure behavior, and existing zero-mutation coverage.

Used a read-only sidecar to confirm the intended PM Lane 163 structure, test coverage, and no-write/no-new-export guardrails while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
validation: rg -n "PM Lane 163|Field prep output subgroups|Field Prep Basics|Admission Drafts|Pilot Launch Outputs|mutationRequests\)\.toHaveLength\(0\)" PROJECT_STATUS.md docs/operations ops/agents apps/operations-web
validation_result: PASS - operations-web typecheck passed; operations-web build passed; focused Playwright pm import-intake smoke passed; packet JSON parsed; PM Lane 163 guardrail rg passed; git diff --check passed with only expected CRLF warnings on touched and unrelated markdown files.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar Hypatia confirmed that keeping all 19 buttons inside Field Prep Outputs and subgrouping them into 6 Field Prep Basics, 8 Admission Drafts, and 5 Pilot Launch Outputs preserves the existing contract without implying new authority.
next_recommendation: Keep PM live writes blocked. The next safe PM lane can either continue local-only UI ergonomics around review-return use, consume Desktop Codex closeouts for technical-authority review, or prepare explicit PM Lane 142 admission review context without adding persistence, imports, assignments, schedule/status writes, customer commitments, or hosted writes.
